channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Comelec approves synchronization of Naga City plebiscite with BSKE elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has approved the request of Naga City Mayor Leni Robredo to synchronize the plebiscite on converting the city into a highly-urbanized city with the Barangay and S...

item.title: Ukraine faces political and military crisis as Russian strikes kill four civilians
item.description: Russian strikes on Ukraine overnight killed at least four civilians and wounded 20 others, according to President Volodymyr Zelensky, who is navigating a political crisis after dismissing his popular ...

item.title: Roosters edge ahead as Storm's injury woes hamper progress
item.description: The Sydney Roosters have taken an early lead against the Melbourne Storm in an NRL clash, with the Storm facing multiple injuries to their squad. The match, which began with the Storm kicking off, saw...

item.title: Former AP Officer Convicted of Murder After Shooting Colleague in Samburu
item.description: A former Administration Police (AP) officer, Patrick Marwa, has been sentenced to 40 years in prison after being convicted of murdering his colleague, Dancun Cheruiyot, during a disciplinary dispute i...

item.title: PPP Chairman Bilawal Committed to Representing AJK's People in Federal and Global Forums
item.description: PPP Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari addressed a public rally in Dadyal, Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK), where he pledged to serve as the voice of AJK's people in the federal government and globally. His...

item.title: Roosters extend lead with Tupou try in NRL clash against Storm
item.description: The Roosters maintained their dominance in a tightly contested NRL clash against the Storm, with Daniel Tupou's try proving pivotal in securing an eight-point victory. Despite the Storm's injury woes,...

item.title: Tanzanian executive leads Bolt's African corporate mobility expansion
item.description: Ride-hailing giant Bolt has promoted Milu Kipimo, a Tanzanian executive, to Regional Manager for Africa at Bolt for Business. This move marks a strategic shift as the company focuses on expanding its ...

item.title: City Closes Mobile Home Parks Amid Financial Struggles, Residents Face Relocation Challenges
item.description: The city of Bell, California, has announced plans to close several mobile home parks due to financial constraints, leaving long-term residents in a precarious situation. Officials cited the need to en...

item.title: Ukraine Faces Dual Challenges: Russian Strikes and Political Turmoil
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced that Russian attacks overnight killed at least four civilians and wounded 20 others, escalating tensions amid a political crisis triggered by his decis...

item.title: UK-EU trade tensions escalate amid Brexit-related disputes
item.description: The article details the growing political and economic tensions between the United Kingdom and the European Union following the Brexit referendum. Key issues include disputes over trade agreements, re...

item.title: Insurgent Campaign Collapse: Vetting Failures Expose Risks in Recruiting Political Outsiders
item.description: The article details the downfall of Maine gubernatorial candidate Paul Platner, whose campaign collapsed due to inadequate vetting by his consultants. Despite initial efforts by a research firm to scr...

item.title: Political violence and the erosion of democratic norms after Ann Widdecombe's death
item.description: The article reflects on the tragic death of Ann Widdecombe, a prominent UK politician, and examines its implications for British political culture. It critiques the normalization of violence against p...

item.title: Chidimma Adetshina faces deportation challenge amid citizenship dispute
item.description: Former Miss Universe Nigeria and Miss South Africa finalist Chidimma Adetshina is contesting deportation proceedings after being detained in Cape Town for residing illegally. The case stems from alleg...

item.title: Trump's Claims of Chinese Election Interference and Legislative Push
item.description: US President Donald Trump delivered a primetime address alleging that China meddled in the 2020 US elections, claiming intelligence data revealed the theft of 220 million voter files. The speech, whic...

item.title: Gachagua Criticizes Kindiki's Insincere Congratulatory Remarks After Ol Kalou By-Election Victory
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has publicly dismissed Deputy President Kithure Kindiki's congratulatory remarks to Ol Kalou MP-elect Sammy Douglas Kamau as insincere, accusing Kindiki of hyp...

item.title: Philippine Legislators Condemn China's Racist State Media Video Depicting Filipinos as Monkeys
item.description: The article details how Philippine legislators, including Leila de Lima and Sherwin Gatchalian, have condemned China's state media for a viral video that racially caricatured Filipinos as monkeys. The...

item.title: Power Companies Request Fuel Cost Adjustment Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: Power companies in Pakistan have requested a Rs1.20 per unit increase in fuel cost charges for consumers across the country in August, citing higher expenses from imported fuels. Despite 75% of electr...

item.title: Kerala college suspends 13 students over ragging incident involving first-year student
item.description: Thirteen senior students of Government College Kasaragod in Kerala have been suspended for allegedly ragging and assaulting a first-year degree student on the college campus on July 13, 2026. The inci...

item.title: INEC to Conduct Mock Election for 2027 Presidential Polls to Enhance Credibility
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) is considering holding a nationwide mock presidential election before the 2027 general elections to improve credibility, cybersecurity, and public ...

item.title: Rights Groups Challenge Ban on Political Rallies in Tanzania
item.description: Rights groups in Tanzania have filed a legal challenge against Home Affairs Minister Patrobas Katambi's directive that suspended political rallies across the country. The Legal and Human Rights Centre...
